question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that shouldDownload() unconditionally returns true (unlike the parent Downloader which checks downloaderProperties.isEnabled()) to force the importer to continuously poll and download even when the operator intends to pause, causing sustained unnecessary network and CPU load that degrades mirror node responsiveness?
